text.title: Mandatory Rules and AI-Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Concrete rules for the Spring Boot layered pattern and common AI-agent mistakes that break it.

list.item: DTOs are Java records (Java 17+); never return entities from controllers.
All list endpoints accept Pageable; reject unbounded findAll() on growing tables.
Hash passwords with PasswordEncoder (BCrypt) inside the service; never accept the hash from the client.
Run integration tests against a real DB via Testcontainers; never ship H2-only tests.
Use MapStruct annotation processor in pom.xml; hand-coding mappers creates drift.

antipattern.description: Controller returns entity type; @JsonIgnore added to fields to fix lazy loading.
antipattern.reason: @JsonIgnore is a workaround; DTOs are the fix. Entity exposure leaks internal schema and Hibernate proxy state.

antipattern.description: @Transactional missing on write methods in service.
antipattern.reason: Multi-step writes rely on auto-commit per JDBC call; partial failure leaves inconsistent state with no rollback.
Full table scan on large tables causes OOM and timeouts. Pagination is required on every list endpoint.

antipattern.description: MapStruct annotation processor missing from pom.xml.
antipattern.reason: Mapper interface fails to generate at compile time — manifests as a mysterious "interface cannot be instantiated" error at runtime.

antipattern.description: Spring Boot 3 jakarta.* imports mixed with Spring Boot 2 javax.* imports.
antipattern.reason: Namespace migration broke compatibility; mixing produces compile errors or subtle runtime failures.

antipattern.description: Self-invocation: calling @Transactional method on 'this' inside the same bean.
antipattern.reason: Spring AOP proxy is bypassed on self-invocation; the inner @Transactional does not start a new transaction.
